title: Computer Linker config.json
description: Durable Computer Linker configuration. Normal setup commands write this file automatically; the schema is for manual editing, service deployments, and diagnostics. scopes[] is the primary config model; workspaces[] is a 0.x compatibility mirror.

machineId.description: Stable generated machine id. Created automatically when omitted.

machineName.description: Human-readable computer name. Defaults to the OS hostname when omitted.

host.description: Local bind host. The default is 127.0.0.1.

port.description: Local HTTP server port. The default is 3939.

publicBaseUrl.description: Public HTTPS origin or base URL used by public URL tunnels.

publicMcpOnly.description: When true, public-host requests expose only /mcp.

ownerToken.description: Bearer token for HTTP MCP/API access. Keep this secret.

scopes.description: Primary configured scopes. Today Computer Linker supports folder scopes; future non-folder scopes will be added here.

id.description: Stable scope id used by MCP clients.

path.description: Folder path exposed by this scope. ~ is expanded by Computer Linker.

read.description: Allow file reads and read-only project inspection.

write.description: Allow file creation, edits, moves, and deletes inside the configured scope.

shell.description: Allow command and package-script execution inside the configured scope.

codex.description: Allow Codex operations inside the configured scope.

screen.description: Allow screenshot operations when a runtime provider is available.

permissions.description: Permission flags for one folder-backed scope.

maxRuntimeSeconds.description: Maximum runtime for command-like operations in seconds.

maxOutputBytes.description: Maximum captured stdout/stderr bytes for command-like operations.

allowedCommands.description: Command allow patterns such as npm *, pnpm *, node *, or git *.

deniedCommands.description: Command deny patterns that take precedence over allowedCommands.

allowedPackageScripts.description: package.json script-name allow patterns such as test, build, or build:*. When absent, package scripts keep legacy command-policy-only behavior.

deniedPackageScripts.description: package.json script-name deny patterns such as deploy, publish, or release:*. These take precedence over allowedPackageScripts.

allowShellMetacharacters.description: Allow command chaining, pipes, redirects, and shell metacharacters before allowlist evaluation.

allowSensitivePathMetadata.description: Allow list/tree/stat style metadata for secret-like path names.

allowSensitivePathWrites.description: Allow write, patch, move, and delete operations for secret-like paths.

policy.description: Optional execution and sensitive-path policy for one scope.

type.description: Scope provider type. Future non-folder scope types will live in scopes[] next to folder scopes.

items.description: A folder-backed Computer Linker scope. This is the primary durable scopes[] shape.

workspaces.description: 0.x compatibility mirror of folder scopes. New Computer Linker writes keep this synchronized for older clients.

id.description: Stable scope id used by MCP clients.

path.description: Folder path exposed by this scope. ~ is expanded by Computer Linker.

read.description: Allow file reads and read-only project inspection.

write.description: Allow file creation, edits, moves, and deletes inside the configured scope.

shell.description: Allow command and package-script execution inside the configured scope.

codex.description: Allow Codex operations inside the configured scope.

screen.description: Allow screenshot operations when a runtime provider is available.

permissions.description: Permission flags for one folder-backed scope.

maxRuntimeSeconds.description: Maximum runtime for command-like operations in seconds.

maxOutputBytes.description: Maximum captured stdout/stderr bytes for command-like operations.

allowedCommands.description: Command allow patterns such as npm *, pnpm *, node *, or git *.

deniedCommands.description: Command deny patterns that take precedence over allowedCommands.

allowedPackageScripts.description: package.json script-name allow patterns such as test, build, or build:*. When absent, package scripts keep legacy command-policy-only behavior.

deniedPackageScripts.description: package.json script-name deny patterns such as deploy, publish, or release:*. These take precedence over allowedPackageScripts.

allowShellMetacharacters.description: Allow command chaining, pipes, redirects, and shell metacharacters before allowlist evaluation.

allowSensitivePathMetadata.description: Allow list/tree/stat style metadata for secret-like path names.

allowSensitivePathWrites.description: Allow write, patch, move, and delete operations for secret-like paths.

policy.description: Optional execution and sensitive-path policy for one scope.

items.description: A folder-backed Computer Linker scope without the scope type field. This is the 0.x workspaces[] compatibility shape.
